Leather is the Python charting library for those who need charts *now* and don't care if they're perfect.

Leather isn't picky. It's rough. It gets dirty. It looks sexy just hanging on the back of a chair. Leather doesn't need your accessories. Leather is how Snake Plissken would make charts.

Get it?

Important links:

- Documentation:    <https://leather.rtfd.io>
- Repository:       <https://github.com/wireservice/leather>
- Issues:           <https://github.com/wireservice/leather/issues>

classes.name: leather.scales.ordinal.Ordinal

methods.name: leather.scales.ordinal.Ordinal.contains
documentation:
Return {code}`True` if a given value is contained within this scale's
displayed domain.

documentation: Project a value in this scale's domain to a target range.

methods.name: leather.scales.ordinal.Ordinal.project_interval
documentation:
Project a value in this scale's domain to an interval in the target
range. This is used for places {class}`.Bars` and {class}`.Columns`.

methods.name: leather.scales.ordinal.Ordinal.ticks
documentation: Generate a series of ticks for this scale.
A scale that maps individual values (e.g. strings) to a range.

classes.name: leather.grid.Grid

methods.name: leather.grid.Grid.add_one
documentation: Add a {class}`.Chart` to the grid.

methods.name: leather.grid.Grid.add_many
documentation: Add a sequence of charts to this grid.

methods.name: leather.grid.Grid.to_svg
documentation:
Render the grid to an SVG.

The {code}`width` and {code}`height` arguments refer to the size of the
entire grid. The size of individual charts will be inferred
automatically.

See {meth}`.Chart.to_svg` for arguments.

A container for a set of {class}`.Chart` instances that are rendered in a
grid layout.

functions.name: leather.axis.tick_format_function
documentation:
This example shows how to define a function to format tick values for
display.

```
param x

The value to be formatted.

param index

The index of the tick.

param tick_count

The total number of ticks being displayed.

returns

A stringified tick value for display.
```

methods.name: leather.series.category.CategorySeries.categories
documentation: Return all unique values in the category field.

A series of categorized data and its associated metadata.

Series object does not modify the data it is passed.

```
param data

A sequence (rows) of sequences (columns), a.k.a. 
format. If the  and  are not specified then the first
column is used as the X values and the second column is used for Y.

Or, a sequence of (rows) of dicts (columns), a.k.a.
 format. If this format is used then 
and  arguments must specify the columns to be charted.

Or, a custom data format, in which case  and  must
specify .

param x

If using sequence row data, then this may be either an integer index
identifying the X column, or a .

If using dict row data, then this may be either a key name identifying
the X column, or a .

If using a custom data format, then this must be a
.`

param y

See .

param z

See . This variable identifies the category/sub-series of each
row.

param name

An optional name to be used in labeling this series. This will be
used as the chart title if rendered in a .
```

documentation: Set the X {class}`.Scale` for this lattice.

documentation: See {meth}`.Lattice.set_x_scale`.

methods.name: leather.lattice.Lattice.add_x_scale
documentation:
Create and add a {class}`.Scale`.

If the provided domain values are {class}`date` or {class}`datetime`
then a {class}`.Temporal` scale will be created, otherwise it will
{class}`.Linear`.

If you want to set a custom scale class use {meth}`.Lattice.set_x_scale`
instead.

methods.name: leather.lattice.Lattice.add_y_scale
documentation: See {meth}`.Lattice.add_x_scale`.

methods.name: leather.lattice.Lattice.add_x_axis
documentation:
Create and add an X {class}`.Axis`.

If you want to set a custom axis class use {meth}`.Lattice.set_x_axis`
instead.

methods.name: leather.lattice.Lattice.add_y_axis
documentation: See {meth}`.Lattice.add_x_axis`.

methods.name: leather.lattice.Lattice.add_one
documentation:
Add a data series to this lattice.

```
param data

A sequence of data suitable for constructing a ,
or a sequence of such objects.

param x

See .

param y

See .

param title

A title to render above this chart.
```

methods.name: leather.lattice.Lattice.add_many
documentation:
Same as {meth}`.Lattice.add_one` except {code}`data` is a list of data
series to be added simultaneously.

See {meth}`.Lattice.add_one` for other arguments.

Note that {code}`titles` is a sequence of titles that must be the same
length as {code}`data`.

methods.name: leather.lattice.Lattice.to_svg
documentation:
Render the lattice to an SVG.

See {class}`.Grid` for additional documentation.

A grid of charts with synchronized shapes, scales, and axes.

Lattice only supports graphing a single series of data.

```
param shape

An instance of  to use to render all series. Defaults
to  if not specified.
```

classes.name: leather.ticks.score.ScoreTicker

methods.name: leather.ticks.score.ScoreTicker._find_ticks
documentation: Implements the tick-finding algorithm.

methods.name: leather.ticks.score.ScoreTicker._score
documentation:
Score a given tick sequence based on several criteria. This method returns
discrete scoring components for easier debugging.

methods.name: leather.ticks.score.ScoreTicker._round_tick
documentation:
Round a tick to 0-3 decimal places, if the remaining digits do not
appear to be significant.

Attempt to find an optimal series of ticks by generating many possible
sequences and scoring them based on several criteria. Only the best
tick sequence is returned.

Based an algorithm described by Austin Clemens:
<http://austinclemens.com/blog/2016/01/09/an-algorithm-for-creating-a-graphs-axes/>

See {meth}`.ScoreTicker.score` for scoring implementation.

```
param domain_min

Minimum value of the data series.

param domain_max

Maximum value of the data series.
```

documentation: Set the X {class}`.Scale` for this chart.

methods.name: leather.chart.Chart.set_y_scale
documentation: See {meth}`.Chart.set_x_scale`.

methods.name: leather.chart.Chart.add_x_scale
documentation:
Create and add a {class}`.Scale`.

If the provided domain values are {class}`date` or {class}`datetime`
then a {class}`.Temporal` scale will be created, otherwise it will
{class}`.Linear`.

If you want to set a custom scale class use {meth}`.Chart.set_x_scale`
instead.

methods.name: leather.chart.Chart.add_y_scale
documentation: See {meth}`.Chart.add_x_scale`.

methods.name: leather.chart.Chart.set_x_axis
documentation: Set an {class}`.Axis` class for this chart.

methods.name: leather.chart.Chart.set_y_axis
documentation: See {meth}`.Chart.set_x_axis`.

methods.name: leather.chart.Chart.add_x_axis
documentation:
Create and add an X {class}`.Axis`.

If you want to set a custom axis class use {meth}`.Chart.set_x_axis`
instead.

methods.name: leather.chart.Chart.add_y_axis
documentation: See {meth}`.Chart.add_x_axis`.

methods.name: leather.chart.Chart.add_series
documentation:
Add a data {class}`.Series` to the chart. The data types of the new
series must be consistent with any series that have already been added.

There are several shortcuts for adding different types of data series.
See {meth}`.Chart.add_bars`, {meth}`.Chart.add_columns`,
{meth}`.Chart.add_dots`, and {meth}`.Chart.add_line`.

methods.name: leather.chart.Chart.add_bars
documentation:
Create and add a {class}`.Series` rendered with {class}`.Bars`.

Note that when creating bars in this way the order of the series data
will be reversed so that the first item in the series is displayed
as the top-most bar in the graphic. If you don't want this to happen
use {meth}`.Chart.add_series` instead.

methods.name: leather.chart.Chart.add_columns
documentation: Create and add a {class}`.Series` rendered with {class}`.Columns`.

methods.name: leather.chart.Chart.add_dots
documentation: Create and add a {class}`.Series` rendered with {class}`.Dots`.

methods.name: leather.chart.Chart.add_line
documentation: Create and add a {class}`.Series` rendered with {class}`.Line`.

methods.name: leather.chart.Chart._validate_dimension
documentation:
Validates that the given scale and axis are valid for the data that
has been added to this chart. If a scale or axis has not been set,
generates automated ones.

methods.name: leather.chart.Chart.to_svg_group
documentation:
Render this chart to an SVG group element.

This can then be placed inside an {code}`<svg>` tag to make a complete
SVG graphic.

See {meth}`.Chart.to_svg` for arguments.

methods.name: leather.chart.Chart.to_svg
documentation:
Render this chart to an SVG document.

The {code}`width` and {code}`height` are specified in SVG's
"unitless" units, however, it is usually convenient to specify them
as though they were pixels.

```
param path

Filepath or file-like object to write to. If omitted then the SVG
will be returned as a string. If running within IPython, then this
will return a SVG object to be displayed.

param width

The output width, in SVG user units. Defaults to
.

param height

The output height, in SVG user units. Defaults to
.
```

Container for all chart types.

```
param title

An optional title that will be rendered at the top of the chart.
```
